text: Large language models, or LLMs, are advanced AI systems trained on enormous amounts of text data from the internet, books, and other sources. They work by analyzing patterns in language and predicting the next word in a sequence based on context. To use an LLM effectively, people often practice prompt engineering—carefully crafting questions or instructions to get clear, useful responses. LLMs can write essays, answer questions, and even create code, making them powerful tools for many tasks. However, they have limits: they don't truly understand meaning, can generate incorrect or biased information, and rely entirely on their training data, which may be outdated or flawed.
